commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Needs human maintainer acceptance of the SSRF boundary change and public SDK export; there is no narrow automated repair to request.; Needs attention: Needs maintainer security acceptance because the patch deliberately allows a host-scoped fake-IP exception for OpenAI Realtime secret requests. (sha=49b9ba01ffae7b1c55de7acd86893d6a48edef5f)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a host-scoped fake-IP SSRF policy to shared OpenAI Realtime secret creation, exports the helper from `plugin-sdk/ssrf-runtime`, and asserts the policy in Realtime voice/transcription tests.

PR surface: Source +9, Tests +20. Total +29 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source shows current main and `v2026.6.6` send Realtime secret requests through `fetchWithSsrFGuard` without the fake-IP policy, and the supplied screenshots show the before blocked error plus after-fix Realtime RPCs returning `ok: true`.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Needs human maintainer acceptance of the SSRF boundary change and public SDK export; there is no narrow automated repair to request.

target.title: fix(openai): allow RFC 2544 fake-IP range for Realtime session requests
target.body: ## Summary - `fetchWithSsrFGuard` in `createOpenAIRealtimeSecret` was missing a fake-IP SSRF policy, causing the SSRF guard to block OpenAI Realtime session-secret requests when proxy stacks such as Clash, Surge, or sing-box in TUN/fake-IP mode resolve `api.openai.com` to special-use addresses. - The fix reuses `ssrfPolicyFromHttpBaseUrlFakeIpHostnameAllowlist("https://api.openai.com/v1")`, so the relaxation is still scoped to the expected OpenAI hostname. - Added policy assertions to the OpenAI Realtime voice and transcription tests to prevent silent regression. Closes part of #56535. ## Verification ```bash pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check CI=true pnpm test extensions/openai/realtime-voice-provider.test.ts extensions/openai/realtime-transcription-provider.test.ts ``` - `plugin-sdk:api:check`: passed - Test files: 2 passed - Tests: 59 passed ## Real Behavior Proof - Behavior addressed: SSRF guard blocks OpenAI Realtime session-secret requests when a TUN/fake-IP proxy resolves `api.openai.com` to `198.18.0.0/15`. - Real environment tested: macOS 15, OpenClaw 2026.5.25 (`e3b9c11`), Clash TUN/fake-IP mode active. - Exact steps after this patch: `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=~/.openclaw-dev pnpm gateway:dev --port 19001`; open `http://127.0.0.1:19001`; click Realtime Voice. - Evidence after fix: `talk.client.create` returned `ok: true` with `durationMs: 1711`; audio streaming stayed active with continuous `talk.session.appendAudio` calls returning `ok: true`; AI responded in realtime. - Observed result after fix: Realtime voice session fully established, with no SSRF block error. - What was not tested: live Surge / sing-box environments. They use the same code path and policy opt-in. **Browser DevTools — session creation (`talk.client.create` -> `ok: true`):**
target.author: shushushv

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
